factors.name: Post-Holiday Rally Momentum
factors.summary: US stock futures rose on Sunday, with Wall Street looking to extend last week's gains. S&P 500 ES futures +0.4%, NQ +1.2% to open new week trade, indicating broad equity optimism.

factors.name: AI Earnings Momentum (Semis & Dow Components)
factors.summary: Samsung forecast 18-fold profit jump on surging AI memory demand; SK Hynix $29B US listing planned; TSMC receiving Citi PT lift on 2026 guidance raise. These are major suppliers to Dow heavyweights (MSFT, Apple ecosystem).

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran-Hormuz)
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening holding; tankers crossing via US-protected corridor; OPEC+ raising output targets by 188k bpd starting August. Oil volatility easing supports refining margin clarity and reduces cost-push inflation fears.

factors.name: Rate Hike Expectations Cooling
factors.summary: Gold prices rose as cooling rate-hike bets hit dollar; ECB's Moulin signals bank in 'good position' as oil-driven inflation eases; RBNZ decision described as 'line-ball' for July. Lower real rate trajectory supports dividend-heavy Dow valuations.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & FX Headwinds
factors.summary: USD/JPY extends gains to ~161.70; PBOC weakened CNY reference rate to 6.8066 vs 6.7850 estimate; Goldman Sachs cuts JPY forecast to 165/USD. Dow's ~50% international revenue faces translation headwind; strong dollar pressures multinational earnings.

factors.name: Oil Price Overhang & Inventory Refill
factors.summary: OPEC+ output hike despite 'tumbling crude prices'; oil glut weakening Iran's Hormuz leverage; OECD stocks at Dec-1990 lows but refill cycle will take longer than 60-day peace window. Downstream cost pressures may persist for airlines, transportation (Dow exposure via BA, HON).

factors.name: China Macro Softness & FX Volatility
factors.summary: PBOC managing yuan weakness via reference rate weakness and reverse repos; Shanghai sentiment fragile on slowing growth backdrop; Wang Yi seeking EU capital. Caterpillar, Boeing, 3M China exposure vulnerable to slower China PMI ahead.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty & Minutes Pending
factors.summary: Markets awaiting Fed minutes on interest rate trajectory. Rate-cut expectations cooling but not eliminated; key inflation data (CPI, PCE) not yet released in this window. Sentiment fragile into event risk.

factors.name: Earnings Season Imminent
factors.summary: Dow heavyweight earnings coming soon; AI momentum in memory chips likely lifts sentiment for tech-adjacent Dow names. Citi lifts TSMC on 2026 guidance, signaling confidence in capex cycle.

factors.name: Sector Rotation Risk (Asia Chips Weakness)
factors.summary: Japan, South Korea equities leading Asia lower as chipmakers pull back after rebound; Bank of Korea warns on leveraged ETFs in Samsung/SK Hynix. Sentiment fragility in semis post-rally; Dow tech exposure at risk if rotation reverses.

screening.narrative: The Dow opened the week with modest gains (+0.69% in 4h, +0.49% in 24h) on post-holiday optimism and AI-driven upside in semiconductor earnings (Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC). Geopolitical de-escalation (Hormuz reopening, OPEC+ output hike, gold rally on cooling rate-hike bets) supports a relief trade into cyclicals and dividend payers. However, this move is already underway in Phase 2, with Asia-Pacific chipmakers pulling back and dollar strength creating multinational translation headwinds. China FX management (PBOC yuan weakness) and softer macro signals argue for caution. The Dow's heavy weighting toward industrials, financials, and energy makes it vulnerable to: (1) a falter in China growth data, (2) a reversal in dollar strength if geopolitical risk re-erupts, and (3) earnings misses from cyclical leaders (CAT, BA, HON) if cost pressures (oil input, logistics) persist.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes (likely this week) — rate path clarity critical for dividend-heavy Dow valuations
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA, HON in coming weeks) — guidance on capex, margins, China exposure
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI — direct read on Dow's industrial composition
Chinese PMI and macro data — CAT, DE, Boeing demand signal
RBNZ July decision (Wednesday) — if hikes despite soft inflation, risk-on sentiment could spike globally
NZIER rate decision signals — shadow board split, market volatility risk
Oil price action & Hormuz shipping updates — cost input and geopolitical drift
screening.risk_warning: If China PMI rolls over, CAT/DE guidance disappoints, or Dollar Index rebounds sharply (signaling renewed geopolitical risk or rate-hike expectations), the Dow's multinational earnings outlook darkens fast. Conversely, if Fed minutes signal faster rate cuts or Samsung/SK Hynix earnings blowout AI demand, bias flips higher. Move is already +0.7% in 4h (Phase 2); entry should wait for retest of support ~52,500 rather than chase extended highs.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (+0.69% in 4h). Wait for pullback toward 52,500 support or retest of overnight highs (53,100–53,200) before adding. Avoid chasing into resistance; target near-term resistance at 53,200, then 53,500. If China PMI or Fed minutes disappoint, risk reversal to 52,000–52,200.
